claim: Normal text needs 4.5:1 contrast; large text and meaningful non-text UI need 3:1.
method_or_sample: Consensus standard with testable success criteria
applicability: Hard accessibility gate for copy, controls, focus, and meaningful boundaries
limitation: Conformance does not prove aesthetic quality or cognitive clarity
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Decorative lines are exempt; treating every faint separator as a failure would overstate the rule

claim: Content must reflow at 320 CSS px without two-dimensional scrolling or loss, and text must survive 200% resizing.
method_or_sample: 320 CSS px corresponds to 1280 px at 400% zoom
applicability: Hard gate for mobile order and zoom resilience
limitation: Some genuinely two-dimensional content is exempt
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Passing 320 px reflow does not by itself prove a good mobile hierarchy

claim: Pointer targets need at least 24x24 CSS px or sufficient separation; 44x44 is the enhanced target.
applicability: Sets the minimum; Final Prime can use 44 px as its internal default
limitation: Inline links and specified exceptions apply
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Large targets still fail if labels or hierarchy are unclear

claim: Aesthetic reactions to webpages can stabilise after very short exposure.
method_or_sample: Three studies; homepage appeal ratings at 500 ms and 50 ms
applicability: Justifies a controlled first-impression stage before comprehension tasks
limitation: Measured visual appeal, not complete credibility, comprehension, or conversion
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: A visually strong but unclear hero can score well initially and fail the task stage

claim: Visual complexity affects first impressions earlier than prototypicality; low complexity plus high prototypicality was most appealing in the tested set.
method_or_sample: Two studies; 119 real-site screenshots; exposures from 17 to 1000 ms
applicability: Supports reducing competing frames and preserving a recognisable homepage skeleton
limitation: The stimuli and site conventions are dated; appeal is not the same as desired brand distinctiveness
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Excessive prototypicality can make Final Prime generic; distinctiveness must be added inside a clear skeleton

claim: Greater perceptual or conceptual fluency often increases aesthetic pleasure.
method_or_sample: Integrates findings on contrast, symmetry, repetition, priming, and prototypicality
applicability: Supports legible hierarchy beneath the mysterious surface
limitation: This is a broad explanatory framework rather than a homepage experiment
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Too much fluency can remove curiosity and expressive character; fluency should apply to navigation, not eliminate all ambiguity

claim: Curiosity is triggered by a perceived information gap, but the gap must be recognisable enough to matter.
method_or_sample: Review and information-gap reinterpretation of curiosity research
applicability: Supports one controlled unresolved question or signal rather than unexplained jargon
limitation: Not a website-conversion study and does not define an optimal amount of mystery
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: If visitors cannot identify the subject or expected payoff, the gap becomes confusion rather than curiosity

claim: Competence and warmth are distinct, broadly recurring dimensions of social perception.
method_or_sample: Nine varied samples evaluating social groups
applicability: Provides the two-axis test for formidable technical authority and human alignment
limitation: The original object of study was social stereotypes, not website brands
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: High competence with low warmth may produce distance or envy rather than trust; warmth without evidence may look amateur

claim: Users distinguish orderly classical aesthetics from creative expressive aesthetics.
method_or_sample: Multi-stage scale development and validation for website aesthetics
applicability: Maps directly to the need for a stable skeleton plus experimental expression
limitation: Aesthetic dimensions do not prescribe a particular layout or predict business outcomes
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Optimising only classical order makes the site generic; optimising only expression makes it harder to parse

claim: Website aesthetics can be measured through simplicity, diversity, colorfulness, and craftsmanship.
method_or_sample: Seven studies validating the VisAWI facets
applicability: Provides a structured rubric for benchmark and prototype evaluation
limitation: Self-report aesthetics remain subjective and culturally situated
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: A high overall score can hide a fatal weakness in one facet or in task comprehension
confidence: High for measurement; medium for prediction

claim: The four-item VisAWI-S approximates overall website-aesthetic judgments and relates to revisit intention.
applicability: Useful in a short 12-16 person test without exhausting participants
limitation: The local sample is far too small for population estimates
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Treat scores as directional and combine them with recall and task evidence
confidence: High for instrument; low-medium for this small sample

claim: For identical content, higher aesthetic treatment increased perceived credibility in 19 of 21 tested page pairs.
method_or_sample: 21 landing-page pairs with unchanged content and reduced visual treatment
applicability: Shows that visual craft is credibility infrastructure, not decoration
limitation: Researchers judged treatment level; the sites and design conventions are dated
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Aesthetic polish can temporarily mask weak proof; credibility must also come from truthful work records

claim: Color can affect cognition and behaviour, but meanings depend on context and the literature does not support universal application recipes.
method_or_sample: Review of achievement, affiliation, attraction, consumer, and consumption contexts
applicability: Requires cyan and fuchsia to receive consistent semantic roles inside this site
limitation: Boundary conditions, moderators, and real-world generalisability remain underdeveloped
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Claims such as cyan equals trust or fuchsia equals rebellion must be tested locally, not asserted as psychology
confidence: High for caution; medium for directional effects

claim: Curved contours were preferred over sharp contours in the original controlled object task.
method_or_sample: 140 object pairs including real and meaningless forms
applicability: Supports testing a small amount of softness as a warmth cue against the sharp industrial core
limitation: Object preference is not identical to website or brand perception
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Angular forms may intentionally signal distance, strength, or premiumness; wholesale rounding would contradict the brand

claim: Positive polarity can improve acuity and proofreading performance compared with negative polarity under tested conditions.
method_or_sample: Younger and older adults; visual-acuity and proofreading tasks on TFT displays
applicability: Prevents claiming that dark mode is inherently more readable; long copy needs extra scrutiny
limitation: Task, lighting, age, device, and display conditions limit transfer
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: The dark identity can remain if copy is concise, high-contrast, well-spaced, and empirically readable

claim: Dark interfaces need legibility tests under multiple contrast and display conditions; custom colors should be role-consistent.
method_or_sample: Platform design guidance, not an experiment
applicability: Useful operational checklist for dark surfaces, semantic colors, typography, and increased contrast
limitation: App-platform guidance is informative rather than normative for a website
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Blindly copying system colors would erase the brand; retain custom colors but test them

claim: Vertical symmetry and visual complexity strongly affect simplicity, diversity, and craftsmanship; color factors especially affect colorfulness.
method_or_sample: Five online experiments using real-site screenshots; total N=194
applicability: Supports separating structural tests from hue, saturation, and brightness tests
limitation: Screenshot judgments do not capture interaction or long-term use
counterevidence_or_failure_mode: Changing color cannot repair structural overload; changing structure can still harm expressive character
